concepts.title: Hopf algebras
concepts.blurb: A Hopf algebra packages multiplication $m$, unit $\eta$, comultiplication $\Delta$, counit $\varepsilon$, and antipode $S$ with compatibility axioms; the group ring $\mathbb{C}[G]$ with $\Delta(g)=g\otimes g$ and $S(g)=g^{-1}$ is the prototypical example, while $U(\mathfrak g)$ is the cocommutative Lie counterpart.

concepts.title: Drinfeld–Jimbo $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$
concepts.blurb: Generators $E,F,K^{\pm 1}$ with $KEK^{-1}=q^2 E$, $KFK^{-1}=q^{-2} F$, and $[E,F]=(K-K^{-1})/(q-q^{-1})$ define the quantized enveloping algebra; the parameter $q\to 1$ recovers $U(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ as a deformation.

concepts.title: Universal $R$-matrix and Yang–Baxter
concepts.blurb: $U_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is quasitriangular: there is $R\in U_q(\mathfrak{g})\otimes U_q(\mathfrak{g})$ intertwining $\Delta$ with its opposite and satisfying the Yang–Baxter equation $R_{12}R_{13}R_{23}=R_{23}R_{13}R_{12}$ — the algebraic engine behind braid statistics.

concepts.title: Representations and crystal bases
concepts.blurb: For $q$ generic, finite-dimensional $U_q(\mathfrak g)$-modules of type 1 are parametrized by dominant weights exactly as in the classical case; Lusztig's canonical basis and Kashiwara's crystal basis specialize at $q=0$ to a coloured graph encoding tensor decompositions combinatorially.

concepts.title: Reshetikhin–Turaev knot invariants
concepts.blurb: Assigning the $R$-matrix to each crossing turns a labelled tangle diagram into a $U_q(\mathfrak{g})$-equivariant operator; the resulting trace is a polynomial knot invariant — Jones from $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, HOMFLY from $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_n)$, Kauffman from $U_q(\mathfrak{so}_n)$.

concepts.title: Affine, KZ, and categorification
concepts.blurb: Affine quantum groups $U_q(\widehat{\mathfrak g})$ govern integrable lattice models and the Knizhnik–Zamolodchikov equations whose monodromy is the $R$-matrix (Drinfeld–Kohno); quantum geometric Langlands and Khovanov–Lauda–Rouquier categorification lift these structures to derived and 2-categorical settings.
